activities_projects.title: Periodisation Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Understand different ways historians divide history
steps: Write the three periods suggested by James Mill
Write the modern classification used by historians
Compare the two systems and write one difference

activities_projects.title: Administration Records Study
activities_projects.objective: Understand how official records help historians
steps: List examples of administrative records (reports, letters, surveys)
Write how these records help historians understand the past
Explain one advantage of such records

activities_projects.title: Colonial Rule Investigation
steps: Write the definition of colonialism
List two changes brought by British rule in India
Explain how these changes affected society

activities_projects.title: Historical Sources Chart
steps: Divide the chart into two columns: written sources and visual sources
Write examples under each category
Explain what information each type provides

activities_projects.title: Governor-General Timeline
steps: Write names of four Governor-Generals mentioned in the chapter
Arrange them in chronological order
Write one policy or action of any one of them

activities_projects.title: Debate on Periodisation
activities_projects.objective: Encourage critical thinking about historical divisions
steps: Discuss why historians divide history into periods
Write two advantages of periodisation
Write one problem with dividing history this way

activities_projects.title: Understanding Colonial Economy
activities_projects.objective: Identify economic changes during colonial rule
steps: Write how the British controlled India's economy
List two examples of economic policies
Explain how they affected local people

activities_projects.title: Advertisement Analysis
steps: Look at an old advertisement image in the chapter
Write what product is shown
Explain what it tells us about society and markets
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns to interpret visual historical sources

activities_projects.title: Historical Questions Activity
steps: Write three questions about the past
Write what sources could answer each question
Explain why those sources are useful
activities_projects.evaluation: Relevance of questions and sources (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Mini Research Task
steps: Choose one topic from the chapter
Write five key points about it
Present your findings in class
